prose:
**Nitrobenzene Fukui reactivity analysis** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier standard, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied xyz (single-point evaluation, not re-optimized). Partial charges computed via Mulliken scheme from SCF of neutral, cation (+1), and anion (−1) states. **Convergence:** all three states converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000). **Integrity check:** trustworthy = true.

**Key findings:** The most electrophilic site is the ortho carbon (C4, atom index 3), with f⁺ = 0.4964 and dual descriptor = 0.5107, reflecting nitro-group deactivation and ortho/para-directing effect. The most nucleophilic site is the meta carbon (C5, atom index 4), with f⁻ = 0.1073. The nitro-group oxygens show high f⁺ (≈0.47), indicating nucleophilic preference at N–O bonds. Complete per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) for all 14 atoms reported in the table above. A visualization plot was generated and saved to the output directory.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.49641781
